title: Kalbaisakhi brings respite from sweltering heatwave in Odisha, weather pattern to continue for 4 more days - Odisha TV
full_text:
Kalbaisakhi rain is expected to continue across Odisha for the next four days. The rain is likely to bring relief from the prevailing heat. Thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds are also forecasted for several districts.
Regional Meteorological Centre in Bhubaneswar forecasted continued Kalbaisakhi influence across the state, weather conditions will turn unstable during the afternoon and evening hours after hot and humid mornings.
Thunderstorm Alerts
Bhubaneswar Met office issued a yellow warning for thunderstorms and lightning in 24 districts on Sunday.
A similar alert has been issued for 23 districts on Sunday, with the possibility of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds in several parts of the state.
The current weather pattern is likely to continue marked by intense heat and humidity during the first half of the day, followed by development of dark clouds and rain later in the afternoon.
Sharp Drop In Bhubaneswar Temperature
Bhubaneswar witnessed heavy rainfall and strong winds on Saturday which resulted in a significant fall in temperature.
The city’s temperature, which had touched around 40 degrees Celsius during the morning, dropped to 26 degrees Celsius after the rain, marking a decline of 14 degrees Celsius within a short span.
The heat index also registered a sharp fall. While it had reached 50 degrees Celsius before the rainfall, it dropped to 28 degrees Celsius afterwards.
Similar Weather Likely To Continue
According to the Meteorological Department, weather conditions similar to those experienced on Saturday are likely to prevail on Sunday as well.

The continuing Kalbaisakhi activity is expected to moderate temperatures in several areas, although hot and humid conditions are likely to persist during the morning hours before thunderstorms develop later in the day.
